name: Citizens Guaranty Bank
description_short: Citizens Guaranty Bank is an FDIC-insured community bank serving Kentucky with checking, savings, loans, and credit card products through multiple local branches.
description_long: Citizens Guaranty Bank (CG Bank) operates as a traditional community bank with FDIC insurance backing, serving customers primarily in Kentucky with physical locations in Richmond, Berea, Irvine, and Stanton. The bank emphasizes local relationship banking with the tagline "My Money. My Bank. My CGB," positioning itself as a rooted community institution. CG Bank offers a comprehensive suite of personal and business banking products. On the deposit side, they provide multiple checking accounts (Kasasa Cash Checking with 2.0% APY up to $25,000, Kasasa Cash Back Checking with 4% cash back, Citizens Checking with no minimum balance, and specialty accounts like Clean Slate and Senior Checking), savings accounts including Kasasa Saver, money market accounts, CDs, and IRAs. Their credit products include personal loans, home mortgages, home equity lines, auto loans, business checking and savings accounts, commercial real estate loans, construction loans, agriculture/farm loans, and credit cards. Additional services include digital banking, digital wallet, Mastercard debit cards (including college-branded options), overdraft services, check reordering, and Coinstar coin conversion. CG Bank distinguishes itself through the Kasasa product line, which offers competitive rates and cash rewards tied to account qualification requirements. They emphasize accessibility (AudioEye-enabled website), 24/7 digital banking, local decision-making, and community-focused lending including agricultural financing. The bank positions itself as a personal alternative to larger regional institutions while maintaining comprehensive lending capabilities including commercial real estate and construction loans. As a traditional brick-and-mortar community bank, CG Bank's reach is geographically limited to Kentucky locations. While they offer competitive rates on deposit products and comprehensive services, customers outside their service area cannot access physical branch services. The bank does not advertise premium features like investment services, wealth management, or advanced business banking tools typical of larger institutions. Their strength lies in personalized community banking rather than digital-first innovation.
services: Personal checking accounts (Kasasa Cash, Kasasa Cash Back, Citizens, Clean Slate, Senior, Interest checking)
Personal savings accounts (Kasasa Saver, Citizens Savings) and Money Market Accounts
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) and Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs)
Business checking and savings accounts with multiple tiers
Personal loans, auto loans, and home mortgage/equity loans
Commercial real estate loans, construction loans, and business lines of credit
Credit cards and Mastercard debit cards (including college-branded options)
Digital banking, digital wallet, and 24/7 mobile access
Overdraft services and ATM fee refunds
Check reordering, lost/stolen card services, and Coinstar coin conversion
pros: Kasasa Cash Checking offers 2.0% APY on balances up to $25,000 with ATM fee refunds
Kasasa Cash Back Checking provides 4% cash back on debit card purchases (up to $8/month)
FDIC-insured accounts backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
No minimum balance requirement on Citizens Checking account
24/7 digital banking with mobile check deposit and fund transfers
Comprehensive lending products including agriculture/farm loans and construction financing
Local decision-making and community-focused banking approach across four Kentucky locations
Specialized account options for seniors and those rebuilding credit (Clean Slate Checking)
cons: Geographic limitation: only four branches in Kentucky (Richmond, Berea, Irvine, Stanton), restricting physical access for non-local customers
Kasasa Cash APY rate (2.0%) requires monthly qualification criteria; drops to 0.03% if not met
No mention of investment services, wealth management, or advisory services
Limited digital-first features compared to neobanks and major online banks
Branch closure notice mentioned on website suggests recent consolidation or service reduction
best_for: Kentucky residents seeking local community banking with competitive deposit rates
Small business owners needing agricultural lending or construction financing
Customers who value in-person relationships and local loan decision-making
Depositors willing to meet account qualification requirements for higher APY rewards
diagnosis: Citizens Guaranty Bank is best for Kentucky residents and business owners seeking personalized community banking with competitive rates on deposit products and local lending expertise. The main caveat is geographic limitation to four Kentucky locations; customers outside this footprint cannot access physical branches and may find limited advantages versus larger regional or digital-first banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
meta_description: FDIC-insured community bank serving Kentucky with checking, savings, loans, and credit cards.
